The system is a transactional backend.
The system processes ATM cash withdrawal requests.
The system processes withdrawal requests securely.
The system includes a minimal administrative console.
The system enforces balance rules.
The system enforces limit rules.
The system flags suspicious activity.
The system guarantees that any failed transaction is fully rolled back.
The system authenticates users by card number before any transaction.
The system authenticates users by PIN before any transaction.
The system locks the account after 3 consecutive failed authentication attempts.
The system declines withdrawals exceeding the account balance.
The system declines withdrawals exceeding the daily limit of 1000 per calendar day.
The system processes each withdrawal atomically.
The system rolls back all steps completely if any step fails.
The system ensures the balance is unchanged after a failed withdrawal.
The system detects suspicious withdrawal patterns.
The system flags transactions for administrator review.
The system lets administrators review flagged transactions.
The system lets administrators lock accounts.
The system lets administrators unlock accounts.
The system lets administrators view transaction histories.
The system records every authentication attempt with a timestamp.
The system records every transaction state change with a timestamp.
The system requires a valid card and corresponding personal identification number to verify a user's identity before any transaction proceeds.
After three incorrect PIN entries in a row, the account is automatically disabled to prevent unauthorized access.
A user must present their bank card and enter their secret code to begin any cash withdrawal operation.
When authentication fails three times consecutively, the user's account becomes locked and no further attempts are allowed.
The verification mechanism checks both the plastic card identifier and the numeric passcode to grant or deny access.
The service prevents any withdrawal that would bring the account balance below zero or exceed the one-thousand-currency-units daily cap.
If a requested cash amount is larger than the available funds or surpasses the twenty-four-hour spending ceiling, the transaction is rejected.
Users cannot take out more money than they have in the account or more than the prescribed one-thousand daily withdrawal limit.
When a withdrawal request would violate either the account balance constraint or the daily quota, the operation is denied entirely.
The enforcement component blocks transactions that exceed the account's current funds or the per-calendar-day maximum of one thousand.
The entire withdrawal sequence is executed as a single indivisible unit, ensuring that if anything goes wrong all changes are reversed immediately.
If any part of the cash dispensing process fails, every preceding step is undone so the account balance stays exactly as it was before.
A user's money is never lost or duplicated due to partial failures, because the system reverts every alteration when an error occurs.
When a withdrawal completes successfully, the balance is updated once; but when it aborts, no net change is recorded.
The transaction is processed in an all-or-nothing fashion, guaranteeing full rollback of any partial work on failure.
The system monitors withdrawal patterns and automatically marks transactions that appear unusual for later human inspection.
When a sequence of cash requests deviates from normal behavior, a flag is attached to the corresponding transaction records.
Administrative personnel can review a list of withdrawals that were flagged as potentially suspicious by the detection logic.
Suspicious activity indicators are generated whenever the withdrawal behavior matches predefined anomalous patterns.
The flagging mechanism outputs a separate set of entries that require manual review because they may indicate fraud.
Administrators have the ability to examine all transactions that have been marked as suspicious and decide on further action.
A system operator can access a dashboard showing flagged operations and choose to approve, investigate, or disregard them.
The administrative console provides a dedicated view of every transaction that the suspicious activity detector has highlighted.
Authorized personnel can look over the details of each flagged withdrawal to determine if it is legitimate or fraudulent.
The review interface presents operators with a list of potentially problematic transactions along with their associated data.
An administrator can disable a user's access or re-enable it through a control in the management panel.
The backend allows administrative users to toggle the active status of any account, either locking it down or unlocking it.
When an account is locked by an admin, it cannot be used for any further transactions until it is unlocked again.
Authorized personnel can change an account's state to blocked or unblocked via the administrative tools.
The lock and unlock functions let operators suspend or restore an account's ability to perform withdrawals.
Administrators can pull up a chronological record of all transactions associated with a particular account.
The system offers a feature enabling authorized users to browse the complete past activity of any account holder.
Using the admin interface, an operator can look at every withdrawal, attempted access, and balance change for a specific account.
Account history retrieval supplies a detailed log of events, including successful and failed transactions, sorted by time.
The administrative console includes a capability to display the full transaction trail for any chosen account.
Every login attempt and each change to a transaction's status is recorded with a precise time marker for later auditing.
The system logs all authentication events and modifications to the state of a transaction, storing the exact moment each occurred.
A timestamp is attached to each record of a PIN entry or a shift in transaction status, creating a verifiable timeline.
All attempts to authenticate and every transition of a withdrawal's status are appended to an immutable audit trail.
The audit log captures the time of each access attempt and each status update, providing a trustworthy history.
